response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js credentials and genuine distributed-systems depth, but the role demands a senior C++ engineer in industrial automation — a domain in which this resume shows almost no overlap. C++ appears only in a 6-month stint at National Instruments (2000–2001) and a bachelor thesis project, both over two decades ago; the skills section omits C++ entirely. There is no evidence of multi-threaded systems programming, industrial protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), IEC 62443 secure development, or any industrial/OT automation context. The candidate's event-driven and distributed-systems experience is real but lives entirely in the web/SaaS layer, which does not substitute for the hard systems-programming and industrial-cybersecurity requirements of this position.
strengths: Genuine event-driven and distributed-systems experience (Sociomantic low-latency micro-services, GISCollective geo-data platform) that maps loosely to message-broker architectures
Strong REST API and full-stack background (Node.js, TypeScript, React) aligns with the advantageous skills section
Long career demonstrating technical breadth, mentoring, workshop facilitation, and ownership across multiple domains
concerns: C++ experience is effectively non-existent in a practical, current sense — last touched professionally in 2001 for under 6 months and absent from the skills list entirely
Zero exposure to industrial automation, OT/ICS protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, MQTT), or IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards
No evidence of low-level systems programming skills required for the role: multi-threaded C++ design, STL, performance-critical embedded or on-premises platform work

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or OT domain experience
